Figure 4: Generational trajectories of CR1 and CR2: PyVaCoAl 128 27 (without rescue) vs. 128 10 (with rescue). In the 128 27 configuration, the per-generation block-voting agreement rate CR1 remains within the extremely narrow range of 0.995–0.999, maintaining “near-perfect search” at individual steps. However, CR2 (the cumulative product of CR1) decays monotonically with each passing generation, dropping to approximately 0.905 at the 56th generation. In the 128 10 configuration with rescue, both CR1 and CR2 are maintained at 1.0 across all generations.
